Bookkeeping and accounting are connected, but they are not the same.

Bookkeeping is the collection, recording, and classification of financial activity. Income. Expenses. Transactions. It is the foundation.

Accounting takes that data and turns it into insight. It looks at trends, ratios, performance, and meaning.

In simple terms, bookkeeping builds the books. Accounting builds the strategy.

Without accurate bookkeeping, accounting cannot deliver reliable guidance.

If you want to better understand what your numbers are really saying, call (305) 819-3675. https://www.instagram.com/reel/DWO8FUoiv_3/
